# criblio_search_source (Resource)

SearchSource Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_search_source" "my_search_source" {
  description = "Primary Cribl HTTP ingest for the Worker Group"
  host = "0.0.0.0"
  id = "in_cribl_http_primary"
  port = 10200
  tls = {
    disabled = false
    min_version = "TLSv1.3"
  }
  type = "cribl_http"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host` (String) Bind address for the listener.
- `id` (String) Source configuration id.
- `type` (String) Protocol discriminator selecting the ingest source protocol and listener shape.

### Optional

- `auth_tokens` (Attributes List) Optional bearer or shared tokens accepted by this source. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--auth_tokens))
- `cribl_api` (String) Absolute path for Cribl HTTP API requests. Used when type is cribl_http.
- `description` (String) Optional description shown in configuration UIs.
- `disabled` (Boolean) When true, the source does not accept new connections.
- `elastic_api` (String) Absolute path for Elasticsearch bulk API requests. Used when type is elastic.
- `metadata` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--metadata))
- `port` (Integer) TCP port when the source type uses a single port field.
- `prometheus_api` (String) Absolute path for Prometheus remote write requests. Used when type is prometheus_rw.
- `splunk_hec_acks` (Boolean) When true, enable Splunk HEC acknowledgements. Used when type is splunk_hec.
- `splunk_hec_api` (String) Absolute path for Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC) API requests. Required when type is splunk_hec.
- `subscriptions` (Attributes List) WEF subscriptions; required when type is wef. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscriptions))
- `tcp_port` (Integer) TCP listen port when the source type uses a dedicated TCP port.
- `tls` (Attributes) TLS material and policy for this listener (shape varies by source type). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tls))
- `udp_port` (Integer) UDP listen port when applicable.

<a id="nestedatt--auth_tokens"></a>
### Nested Schema for `auth_tokens`

Required:

- `token` (String) Secret token string clients must present.

Optional:

- `description` (String) Optional label for this token.
- `enabled` (Boolean) When false, the token is rejected.

<a id="nestedatt--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metadata`

Required:

- `name` (String)
- `value` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--subscriptions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscriptions`

Required:

- `subscription_name` (String) Subscription name exposed to Windows clients.
- `content_format` (String) Content format for delivered events.
- `heartbeat_interval` (Number) Maximum seconds between endpoint check-ins before marking unavailable.
- `batch_timeout` (Number) Seconds to collect events before sending to Cribl.
- `targets` (List of String) DNS names of endpoints that forward these events.

Optional:

- `version` (String) Version UUID for this subscription.
- `id` (String)
- `query_selector` (String) Query builder mode; simple with queries, or xml with xmlQuery.
- `queries` (Attributes List) Path and XPath selector entries when using simple mode. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscriptions--queries))
- `xml_query` (String) XPath query when using xml mode.

<a id="nestedatt--subscriptions--queries"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscriptions.queries`

Required:

- `path` (String)
- `query_expression` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--tls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tls`

Required:

- `disabled` (Boolean) When true, TLS is not terminated on this listener.

Optional:

- `cert_path` (String) Filesystem or cloud path to the certificate PEM.
- `min_version` (String) Minimum TLS protocol version.
- `priv_key_path` (String) Filesystem or cloud path to the private key PEM.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_search_source.my_criblio_search_source
  id = "in_cribl_http_primary"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_search_source.my_criblio_search_source in_cribl_http_primary
```
